name: Oakland Unified School District
districtFact: Oakland Unified publishes a districtwide school calendar with student no-school days for professional development, collaboration, teacher work days, holidays, and major breaks. OUSD also operates elementary, middle, high, preschool, alternative education, independent study, and adult education programs across Oakland, so some program-specific dates should be checked with the school or department.

about.title: About Oakland Unified School District
about.content: Oakland Unified School District serves public school students across Oakland in Alameda County. The district includes neighborhood schools, preschools, TK-8 campuses, middle schools, comprehensive high schools, alternative education programs, independent study, and adult education options. OUSD's own public materials describe a district serving about 34,000 students and more than 6,000 employees across 80+ schools, while California Department of Education profile data may show a higher enrollment figure when charter enrollment is included. Families should treat those figures as context, not as calendar rules.

about.title: Oakland District Profile for Calendar Planning
about.content: OUSD calendar planning is more complex than a single PDF because the district serves a large urban school system with many program types. Families may be coordinating neighborhood schools, dual-language or specialized programs, alternative education, preschool, independent study, adult education, and charter-school calendars in the same city. The districtwide calendar is the anchor for regular student attendance, but transportation registration, enrollment offers, ParentSquare messages, Aeries records, nutrition services, school orientations, athletics, graduation communications, summer learning, and expanded learning programs can all add separate planning dates around the same school year.

about.title: How to Use This OUSD Calendar
about.content: Use this page for regular districtwide student attendance planning: first day, last day, Thanksgiving Break, Winter Break, Spring Break, semester changes, professional development days, teacher work days, and school resume dates. For bell schedules, sports, field trips, school performances, graduation details, independent study, adult education, preschool, or alternative education grading periods, check the individual school calendar, ParentSquare message, Aeries portal, or official school announcement.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for Oakland Families
content: Treat professional development, collaboration, and teacher work days as separate childcare checkpoints because they are no-school days even though they are not full holiday breaks.

Check transportation registration and routing before the first day of school; OUSD publishes transportation information separately from the academic calendar, and late routing may take additional business days after school begins.
If your student is entering OUSD or changing schools, compare the academic calendar with the enrollment offer, waitlist, and acceptance timeline before booking summer travel.
For breaks that overlap meal-access planning, check OUSD Nutrition Services and school menus because the district publishes meal program details separately from the academic calendar.
Use ParentSquare and the Aeries Parent/Guardian Portal for last-mile updates such as forms, contact information, school messages, attendance records, grades, and authorization details.

In Oakland neighborhoods with BART, AC Transit, freeway, bridge, or hill-area commute constraints, plan the first day back after Winter Break and Spring Break as higher-friction pickup and drop-off days.

compareIntro: Compare the traditional calendars for OUSD and nearby California districts. Dates shown include the first day, last day, and spring break; families should also compare professional development days, transportation deadlines, and school-specific events before arranging shared childcare or travel.

districtFaqs.q: Which Oakland Unified calendar does this page summarize?
districtFaqs.a: This page summarizes the official OUSD district school calendar for regular student planning, including first day, last day, holidays, breaks, professional development days, collaboration days, and teacher work days.

districtFaqs.q: Are OUSD professional development and teacher work days no-school days?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. The official calendar labels professional development days, collaboration/work days, and teacher work days as no-school days for students. Families should add them to childcare calendars separately from holiday breaks.

districtFaqs.q: Can Oakland Unified calendar dates change?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. District calendar dates can change after publication. Families should verify critical plans against the official OUSD district calendar page and school-level announcements.

districtFaqs.q: Do all OUSD schools use the same grading calendar?
districtFaqs.a: Not always. OUSD notes that some alternative education high schools operate on different grading calendars. Student attendance holidays and breaks are districtwide unless a school communicates otherwise, but grading-period details should be confirmed with the school.

districtFaqs.q: Does OUSD transportation follow the same deadline as the school calendar?
districtFaqs.a: No. Transportation registration and routing deadlines are separate from the student attendance calendar. Families should check the OUSD transportation page for the applicable school year before relying on a calendar date for bus or specialized transportation planning.

districtFaqs.q: Where do OUSD families check school-level updates?
districtFaqs.a: Families should use official school announcements, ParentSquare, the Aeries Parent/Guardian Portal, and the district calendar page for updates. MySchoolDates summarizes major districtwide calendar dates but does not replace school-level communication.

districtFaqs.q: Does the OUSD calendar include charter schools?
districtFaqs.a: Not necessarily. Oakland has charter and independently operated schools that may publish their own calendars. This page focuses on Oakland Unified districtwide student calendar dates, so families at charter schools should check the school calendar directly.

districtFaqs.q: Do OUSD preschool and adult education programs use this same calendar?
districtFaqs.a: Some dates may align, but preschool, early childhood education, adult education, independent study, and specialized programs can have program-specific schedules. Use the district calendar for regular student planning and confirm program dates with the relevant school or department.

districtFaqs.q: Does Oakland Unified publish summer school or summer learning dates?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. OUSD publishes summer learning information separately from the regular academic calendar, including summer program windows, eligibility steps, and program locations. Families should check the OUSD Summer Learning page before planning summer childcare or credit recovery.

districtFaqs.q: Where should Oakland families check after-school and expanded learning schedules?
districtFaqs.a: OUSD publishes Expanded Learning information for before-school, after-school, summer, and enrichment programs. Those schedules may depend on school site, program provider, and eligibility, so they should be checked separately from the districtwide calendar.

districtFaqs.q: Does the OUSD calendar include graduation requirements or senior deadlines?
districtFaqs.a: No. The district calendar can show the school-year window, but graduation requirements, senior projects, credit recovery, and school-level graduation events should be checked through OUSD high school resources and the student's school.

districtFaqs.q: Where can OUSD families find minimum-day or bell schedule details?
districtFaqs.a: Minimum-day, bell schedule, orientation, and school event details are usually school-level information. Use the school directory, school website, ParentSquare, and Aeries updates rather than assuming those details are included in the districtwide calendar.

customSections.label: OUSD Calendar Types and Programs
customSections.content: Oakland Unified calendar planning often depends on the type of school or program. The district calendar is the right starting point for most students, but several OUSD programs require a second check before families make fixed plans.

groups.label: Which calendar should families check?
items: Districtwide school calendar: use for regular PK-12 attendance dates, holidays, breaks, professional development days, teacher work days, and the first and last day of instruction.
Alternative education high schools: verify grading periods and program-specific dates directly because OUSD notes that some alternative education calendars differ.
Preschool and early childhood programs: check child development center or early childhood notices for program hours, open days, and break coverage.
Independent study and adult education: confirm program calendars separately when a student is not following the standard school-year pattern.
Charter or independently operated schools in Oakland: use the individual school calendar rather than assuming OUSD districtwide dates apply.

customSections.label: OUSD Parent Resource Calendar Notes
customSections.content: Some of the most important planning dates for Oakland families sit outside the academic calendar. Enrollment, transportation, communication tools, and student records can affect family logistics before the first day of school.

groups.label: Calendar-linked family checkpoints
items: Enrollment: check OUSD enrollment timelines, offer dates, waitlist steps, and school assignment notices separately from the academic calendar before booking summer travel.
Transportation: check the OUSD transportation page for the applicable registration and routing deadline before the first day of school.
ParentSquare: OUSD uses ParentSquare for districtwide communications, school messages, forms, alerts, and family updates.
Aeries Parent/Guardian Portal: families use Aeries to view attendance, grades, transcripts for grades 9-12, graduation status, teacher contacts, and authorizations.
Student and Family Handbook: use the handbook for annual notices, student services, optional forms, graduation support, and transportation safety information.

customSections.label: Oakland Calendar Context for Families
customSections.content: Oakland school calendar planning is strongly local. A districtwide no-school day can affect families differently depending on neighborhood, transit access, after-school coverage, and whether a student attends a comprehensive school, alternative program, preschool, or charter school.

groups.label: Why Oakland context matters
items: OUSD schools span East Oakland, West Oakland, North Oakland, the hills, downtown, and other neighborhoods, so commute time can vary widely on the first day back after long breaks.
BART, AC Transit, freeway, and bridge traffic can make resume dates after Winter Break and Spring Break more difficult than ordinary weekdays for pickup and drop-off.
Oakland families often coordinate district breaks with city recreation programs, library schedules, community partners, and after-school providers rather than relying only on the school-year PDF.
Professional development and teacher work days can create single-day childcare needs that are easier to miss than Thanksgiving, Winter, or Spring Break.
Air quality, power outages, labor calendar revisions, and other operational issues can change school communications after a calendar PDF is published.

customSections.label: Oakland Parent Resources Around School Breaks
customSections.content: School breaks can affect more than attendance. Oakland families often need to coordinate meals, transportation, after-school coverage, and school communication tools before long recesses and single-day no-school dates.

groups.label: Resources to check before breaks
items: Nutrition Services and school menus: use these before Winter Break, Spring Break, and summer to understand meal access, menu timing, and program notices.
Transportation: confirm registration and routing well before the first day, especially for students who qualify for bus service or specialized transportation.
Student and Family Handbook: review annual notices, transportation safety, graduation support, optional forms, and student service information.
School directory: use the school page for bell schedules, orientation dates, school events, athletics, performances, and direct contact information.
ParentSquare and Aeries: keep phone numbers, emails, forms, attendance records, and school message access current before long breaks and return dates.

customSections.label: Planning Around Oakland
customSections.content: OUSD calendar planning often intersects with city life in ways a PDF date list cannot show. Use the academic calendar together with summer learning, expanded learning, meal, transit, and school communication resources before making fixed plans.

groups.label: Local planning factors
items: Summer programs: OUSD's Summer Learning page lists program windows, eligibility steps, and school or partner-based opportunities, including TK-8, high school credit recovery, ESY/SPED, and newcomer supports.

Expanded learning: before-school, after-school, summer, and enrichment programs may operate through school sites or community partners, so families should check program schedules separately from the academic calendar.
Meals during breaks: Nutrition Services publishes school menu and summer school meal information, which can matter during long breaks and summer learning windows.
Transit and commute planning: BART, AC Transit, I-880, I-580, bridge traffic, and hill-area routes can make the first day back after long breaks more difficult than ordinary school days.
Airport and travel timing: families using Oakland International Airport or Bay Area weekend travel should compare flight plans with teacher work days, Spring Break, and the last week of school.

Libraries, parks, and community programs: Oakland families often pair no-school weekdays with city recreation, library, museum, and community partner programs, especially when breaks do not align with neighboring districts.

customSections.label: Three-Year OUSD Calendar History
customSections.content: Recent Oakland Unified calendars show why year-specific pages matter. Start dates, last days, Spring Break placement, professional development days, and special make-up or observed days can shift from one school year to the next.

groups.label: Recent year notes
items: 2025-2026: OUSD starts August 11, 2025 and ends May 28, 2026, with Spring Break scheduled April 6-10, 2026.
2026-2027: OUSD starts August 10, 2026 and ends May 27, 2027, with Spring Break moved earlier to March 29-April 2, 2027.
2027-2028: OUSD starts August 9, 2027 and ends May 24, 2028, with Spring Break returning to April 3-7, 2028.
Across the three files, Winter Break stays around late December and early January, while professional development and teacher work days remain important single-day no-school dates.

relatedDistricts.name: San Francisco Unified School District
relatedDistricts.comparisonNote: Nearby Bay Area urban district often compared by families coordinating school breaks and summer planning.

relatedDistricts.name: San Jose Unified School District
relatedDistricts.comparisonNote: Another Bay Area district with published student calendar data and separate local scheduling patterns.
